explanation: Addition operator adds two numbers.
Subtraction operator subtracts numbers.
Multiplication operator multiplies two values.
Division operator returns a float result.
Floor division removes decimal part of result.
Modulo operator gives remainder.
** is the exponentiation operator.
len() returns the number of characters in a string.
lower() converts text to lowercase.
upper() converts text to uppercase.
Curly braces with comma-separated values make a set.
Square brackets make a list in Python.
Curly braces with key:value pairs make a dictionary.
Negative index -1 gives the last element.
and operator returns True only if both sides are True.
or operator returns True if any one condition is True.
range(3) generates numbers 0,1,2 for loop iteration.
sum() adds all elements of a list.
min() returns smallest element.
max() returns largest element.
